name: Lower IGF-1 predicted survival in an exceptional-longevity cohort subset
summary: The nonagenarian cohort adds human survival support for lower IGF-1, while showing sex and disease-history boundaries.
statement: Among 184 nonagenarians from exceptional-longevity families, lower IGF-1 was associated with longer survival in women and in participants with cancer history, but not as a uniform signal across all participants.
population_or_model: Nonagenarians from families enriched for exceptional longevity
time_horizon: Prospective survival follow-up after baseline IGF-1 measurement
quantitative_note: The source reports the strongest association in females and cancer-history subgroups.
caveats: The evidence is observational and may reflect health status, genetics, or survivor bias.
The signal was subgroup-specific and should not be generalized as universal IGF-1 lowering benefit.
The study does not test any intervention.
